value.found_quote: To define your own custom agents, add standalone TOML files under `~/.codex/agents/` for personal agents or `.codex/agents/` for project-scoped agents. Each file defines one custom agent. Codex loads these files as configuration layers for spawned sessions, so custom agents can override the same settings as a normal Codex session config.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes the Custom Agents feature, confirming that users can define their own custom agents with different model configurations (e.g., `model`, `model_reasoning_effort`, `sandbox_mode`) and instructions (via `developer_instructions`) using TOML files placed in `~/.codex/agents/` or `.codex/agents/`. The page also provides concrete TOML file examples. The feature appears to be currently available ("Current Codex releases enable subagent workflows by default"), making its lifecycle status 'active'.
